other_observers: Multiple observers have been reporting this bird for several weeks and may be the same bird found on 12/18/2005 at Long Lake, Larimer.; I am not the original observer.
specific_location: golf course on the north east corner of Mulberry Avenue and Taft Hill Avenue, immediately adjacent to City Park and Sheldon Lake.
description: Small, squat, dark goose associating with larger Canada Geese (Branta canadensis). Head and neck sock, which formed a bib on the upperchest, were solid black, except for the large, black-and-white triangular patches on either side of the neck that connected on the throat but did not on the hindneck. The black neck sock contrasted with the dark brown under- and upperparts. The wing coverts were edged in dull white. The flanks were just beginning to show the large silvery patch shown on adults. Both the under- and uppertail coverts were pure white, completely surrounding the short, black tail. The bill was rather small and entirely black.Behaviors: Associated with a foraging flock of Canada Geese eating grass on a golf course.Call: None heard.Plumage: immature
